items.title: Everton vs Leeds team news LIVE!
Taylor krijgt concurrentie van Maldini bij Lazio
items.summary: Daniel Maldini is maandag in Rome gearriveerd om zijn overstap naar Lazio af te ronden. Daar wordt hij een concurrent van onder meer Kenneth Taylor.

items.title: Celtic to appeal Trusty red at Hearts
items.summary: Celtic will appeal the red card shown to Auston Trusty during Sunday's 2-2 draw at Hearts.

items.title: F1's new era begins with Red Bull and Mercedes making positive starts
items.summary: Formula 1's new rules era formally began in Barcelona on Monday with seven of the grid's 11 teams hitting the track on the opening day of 2026's closed shakedown week in Spain.

items.title: Wiegman: I never expected it to be like this
items.summary: Sarina Wiegman admits to being taken aback by the love and recognition she has received since leading England to their second European Championship.

items.title: Schalke 04 volgt naderende terugkeer van PEC-doelman Schendelaar
items.summary: Jasper Schendelaar (25) heeft een pechjaar. De doelman van PEC Zwolle kreeg een vervelende blessure en daarna een terugslag, maar is op de weg terug. Zijn comeback wordt in de gaten gehouden door Schalke 04.

items.title: Het Nico Dijkshoorn-effect
items.summary: Columnist Nico Dijkshoorn zou graag eens een effect zijn.

items.title: Arteta kan niets met harde kritiek Vieira: 'We waren gewoon slecht'
items.summary: Mikel Arteta heeft gereageerd op kritiek van Patrick Vieira. Het clubicoon hekelde de mentale kracht van Arsenal, dat thuis verloor van Manchester United (2-3). De manager haalt er zijn schouders over op. 'We waren gewoon slecht.'

items.title: Sources: Brazil asks FIFA to host Club World Cup
items.summary: Brazil has asked FIFA to host the 2029 FIFA Club World Cup, sources told ESPN Brazil, saying that the request came during a meeting of top Brazil football officials during a visit to Rio de Janeiro by FIFA president Gianni Infantino on Monday.

items.title: Endrick after hat trick: This is just the beginning
items.summary: Endrick feels vindicated to have moved to Lyon after scoring the first hat-trick of his career in the club's 5-2 league win at Metz.

items.title: Report: Harry Kane in talks for new Bayern deal
items.summary: Harry Kane has opened talks over a new Bayern Munich contract, according to a report in Germany.

items.title: Gotham signs FSU, U.S. U20 star Jordynn Dudley
items.summary: American forward Jordynn Dudley, a two-time NCAA champion with Florida State University, has signed for Gotham FC.

items.title: One week until transfer deadline day - who could be on the move?
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at some of the players that have been linked with moves before the Premier League transfer deadline on 2 February at 19:00 GMT.

items.title: Dit is de stand in de Champions League na zeven speelronden
items.summary: Er zijn inmiddels zeven speelronden achter de rug in de competitiefase van de Champions League 2025/26. PSV en Ajax staan er wisselend voor. Eén ploeg is nog altijd foutloos: Arsenal.

items.title: Mature decision to deny Manchester City a penalty for handball sets example for referees | Chris Foy
items.summary: Farai Hallam trusted his own judgment on Yerson Mosquera incident despite a VAR review and, in doing so, showed the way forward I refereed professional football for 25 years. We were talking about handball when I started and it remains one of the most discussed topics in the game. One reason for this is that we’ve had a number of law changes by the International Football Association Board (Ifab) over recent years. All were made in an effort to achieve consistent outcomes for the benefit of the game, but we can sometimes end up with different interpretations of the laws. Continue reading...

items.title: Sepp Blatter suggests fans should not travel to US for World Cup
items.summary: Ex-Fifa boss shows support for boycott calls Security concerns over tournament have risen Former Fifa president Sepp Blatter on Monday suggested he supports fans boycotting World Cup matches in the United States this year due to security concerns. Blatter gave his support to comments from Swiss anti-corruption lawyer Mark Pieth, who worked with Fifa on potential reforms when Blatter was president, saying fans should stay away from the US for the tournament. Continue reading...

items.title: Are Chelsea genuinely out of the WSL title race in January?
items.summary: Sky Sports columnist Laura Hunter analyses the big talking points from the latest Women's Super League matches, bringing you closer to the key stories at the heart of the women's game.

items.title: Lessons from Man United's victory at Arsenal; Real Madrid win ugly; Liverpool woe; more
items.summary: Man United look better post-Ruben Amorim. Can they keep it going? And how quickly can Arsenal move past Sunday's loss? Gab Marcotti recaps the weekend.

items.title: Replacing a manager midseason is a big call, and not as simple as it sounds | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Liverpool and Tottenham are in different situations but face the same problem: a manager in the hot seat but few ideal options Sign up for Soccer with Jonathan Wilson here Another weekend, another few days of soul-searching for Liverpool and Tottenham. Liverpool had been on a 13-game unbeaten run before Saturday’s defeat to Bournemouth, but nobody could claim a string of results that included home draws with all three promoted clubs was convincing. Spurs had won just two of their 13 league games before Saturday’s away draw at Burnley, which was salvaged only thanks to an injury-time goal from Cristian Romero. For both, European competition had offered some relief – Liverpool looked very good in a 3-0 win away to Marseille while Spurs, at least in the first half, produced probably their best performance since August in beating Borussia Dortmund 2-0 – but the sad truth is that the vast majority of European sides these days simply cannot live with the physicality of the Premier League. That’s not to say that Bournemouth or Burnley are better than Marseille or Dortmund, but it is to say that the challenge they pose a Premier League side is less. Continue reading...

items.title: La Liga’s late, late shows set up a bottom-half battle royale for survival | Sid Lowe
items.summary: If there’s one thing more beautiful than a goal in the 92nd minute it is a goal in the 96th, however ugly it actually is The most romantic line ever written was sprayed on a dirty old wall somewhere in Italy and repeated everywhere else. You’re as beautiful as a goal in the 90th minute, the graffiti goes, and this was as beautiful as it gets until it got better. The board had gone up at the Ciutat de Valencia stadium on Friday night when Elche embarked upon a move that could have come from a cartoon or a console, the final scene in a film. Escape to Victory only more so, it started the way Michael Caine planned it, all arrows and crosses and ping-ping-ping, and finished the way Pelé actually played it: a picture of perfection which earned them a 2-2 draw in the derby at Levante. Or so it goes. From one end to the other Elche had gone, the edge of their area to the heart of Levante’s. There had been a dribble out, a dozen passes, a touch for all of them. A superb assist, three defenders sent the wrong way. And then, two minutes into added time, the finish, Adam Boayar’s astonishing overhead kick sailing into the corner to complete a goal so good it was silly, so pristine as to be almost surreal. As the Ciutat fell silent, teammates piled on and fans in the away corner didn’t so much celebrate as put their hands over their mouths and try not to laugh, barely able to believe this. Continue reading...
